description: Russian hacking group behind the Dridex banking trojan, active since 2009. Operates with a hierarchical structure similar to traditional organized crime, led by Maksim Yakubets from Moscow. Specializes in stealing banking credentials and cryptocurrency through phishing campaigns. The group has been linked to significant financial theft globally.

country_links.evidence_quote: theft attributed to Dridex was estimated at £20 million in the United Kingdom
country_links.context: Theft attributed to Dridex was estimated at £20 million in the United Kingdom, indicating the organization's criminal activity there.
Theft attributed to Dridex was estimated at $10 million in the United States, indicating the organization's criminal activity there.
